concept.title: Key Space View
concept.shortdesc: Browse all DITA keys in your workspace organized by status: defined, undefined, and unused.

section.title: Overview
section.p: The Key Space view in the DitaCraft Activity Bar shows every DITA key across your workspace, grouped by its resolution status. This helps you identify missing key definitions, find unused keys, and navigate to key usages.

section.title: Key Groups
section.p: Keys are organized into three groups:

dlentry.dd: Keys that are both defined in a map (via <keydef> or <topicref keys="...">) and referenced somewhere in your DITA content (via keyref or conkeyref attributes). These are healthy keys that resolve correctly.

Keys that are referenced in your content but have no matching definition in any workspace map. These typically indicate a missing <keydef> element or a typo in a keyref attribute. Shown with a warning icon.
Keys that are defined in a map but never referenced anywhere in your DITA content. These may be candidates for cleanup or may indicate content that needs to use the key.

section.title: Navigating Keys and Usages

ul.li: Click a key: Navigate to the map file where the key is defined
Expand a key: See all files that reference this key via keyref or conkeyref
Click a usage: Navigate directly to the usage location in the editor, with the reference highlighted

section.p: Each key item shows additional details:

ul.li: Description: The target filename for keys with an href, or "(inline)" for keys with inline content
Tooltip: Key name, target file, source map, and scope
Usage description: Reference type (keyref or conkeyref) and line number

section.title: How Keys Are Collected
section.p: DitaCraft builds the key space using a DITA 1.3 spec-compliant BFS traversal:

ol.li: Starting from the root map, traverses the map hierarchy in breadth-first order
Extracts <keydef> and key-bearing <topicref> elements; first definition wins (DITA precedence rule)
Applies @keyscope PushDown inheritance: keys within a scoped branch get scope-qualified aliases (e.g., product-a.install-guide)
Follows keyref chains across scopes to resolve multi-hop references
Records provenance (source line) for every key definition
Enforces a 50,000-entry cap to prevent scope explosion from deeply nested scopes
Scans all .dita, .ditamap, and .bookmap files (up to 500) for keyref and conkeyref attributes
Compares definitions against usages to classify each key into Defined / Undefined / Unused

section.p: For conkeyref values like conkeyref="my-key/element-id", only the key name before the slash is extracted for classification purposes.

section.title: Auto-Refresh
section.p: The Key Space view watches for changes to .ditamap and .bookmap files. When map files are created, modified, or deleted, the view refreshes after a 1000ms debounce period. Key space resolution can be expensive for large workspaces, so the debounce is longer than other views.
To manually refresh, click the refresh button in the view title bar or run DITA: Refresh Key Space from the Command Palette.

ul.li: Key Audit: Review which keys are defined, used, and missing
Cleanup: Identify unused keys that can be removed from maps
Debugging: Find undefined keys causing unresolved references
Navigation: Quickly jump to key definitions or usages across files
